test: FX interface Line A — variance decomposition (real data)
data: user FRED: DEX* FX + OECD CPI (JP,CA,SE,KR,DK) + IR3TIB 3-month rates + US CPI/rate

excluded.JPY: CPI series ends 2021-06, no FX-window overlap

monthly_result.FALSIFICATION_F1: TRIGGERED — monetary share 7.5% < 10% threshold at monthly frequency

horizon_investigation.finding: the near-zero monthly monetary share is a HORIZON ARTIFACT. Inflation-differential R² rises sharply with horizon:
horizon_investigation.interpretation: monthly FX ~ random walk (Meese-Rogoff puzzle); inflation differentials bind at MULTI-YEAR horizons where they explain 20-40% of the cumulative FX move — the horizon that matters for CS (trade competitiveness plays out over years).

honest_verdict: The PRE-REGISTERED monthly test FAILED falsification F1 (monetary share 7.5% < 10%). A horizon analysis shows the share is horizon-dependent and reaches 20-40% at 1-2 years. This is a legitimate methodological finding (PPP is long-horizon, textbook), BUT switching horizon AFTER seeing the result is post-hoc. Clean path: RE-PRE-REGISTER Line A at a multi-year horizon and RE-RUN. Do NOT claim the anchor 'works' on the strength of a horizon switch made after a failed monthly test.
what_this_means_for_the_claim: On the pre-registered test, FX stability is NOT a demonstrable CS advantage at monthly frequency. The long-horizon evidence is promising but must be tested under a fresh pre-registration to count. The audio's 'under-specified, say so honestly' framing remains correct — this does NOT upgrade it.
caveats: DKK anchored to EUR not USD
long-horizon n very small (21-22) — suggestive not conclusive
